Dreidel: A wooden or plastic spinning top with four flat sides. It has a handle on top and a pointed bottom.
Gimel: A Hebrew letter resembling a figure with two branches. It looks similar to the greek letter lambda.
Hay: A Hebrew letter with a horizonal line and two vertical strokes. Note that there is a gap between the top of the horizontal line and the second vertical stroke
Nun: A horizontal line with two vertical strokes. Unlike Hay, there is no gap between the vertical and horizontal lines.
Shin: A letter with three vertical stems connected at the base. It looks like the letter W.
Spinning Dreidel: A dreidel in motion, appearing blurred due to its spin.
